document.title: Software Validation Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
standards: Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices, May 11, 2005

sourceSummary.oneLine: Software validation report for XAS_W software and firmware used with A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by describing software requirements, design, verification, validation, traceability, hazard analysis, revision history, and unresolved anomalies.
keyPoints: The report states that A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW have the same performance and usage except for size, with no difference in software validation.
The XAS_W software performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing for the detector.
The software level of concern is concluded as Moderate under FDA software premarket submission guidance.
Module verification and system validation results are marked pass for listed software, firmware, cybersecurity, and error-control tests.
The unresolved anomalies section lists no remaining anomalies.

evidence.claim: The document is Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-131, Rev. No. 0.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-131 Rev. No. 0 Software Validation Report Rev. Date Nov/23/2022

evidence.claim: The report covers A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: Software Validation Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The covered detector models have the same performance and usage except for size, with no software validation difference.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same except for size, so there is no difference in 'Software Validation' (A-TCF -5-131)

evidence.claim: XAS_W performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing.
evidence.evidenceText: XAS_W is software of Detector that performs image acquisition, image correction, and pre-processing.

evidence.claim: The XAS_W software level of concern is Moderate.
evidence.evidenceText: According to below responds, we conclude a Moderate level of concern applies to the XAS_W software. Level of Concern : Moderate

evidence.claim: The report references FDA guidance for software contained in medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: FDA guidance document entitled “Guidance for the Content of Premarket Submissions for Software Contained in Medical Devices”, May 11, 2005.

evidence.claim: The development process is stated to fulfill 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
evidence.evidenceText: The development process fulfils the requirements 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.

evidence.claim: The software architecture consists of five independent modules.
evidence.evidenceText: Software architecture consists of five independent modules. Acquisition modules provide the ability to correct and process raw images obtained from detectors.

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity requirements include wireless network ID/password access restriction and encryption.
evidence.evidenceText: Random access should be limited by setting ID and password when accessing the wireless network. Transmission data should be protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: Error control includes reconnection support for detector-SDK disconnection and calibration image processing for image noise or line errors.
evidence.evidenceText: It should support recovery mode to enable reconnection in case of disconnection between the detector and SDK software. When noise or line is appeared during the pre-processing the image received from the detector, it should provide a calibration image for normal process.

evidence.claim: Module verification was certified as properly accomplished.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of the code review, we hereby certify that the module verification of Software is properly accomplished.

evidence.claim: System testing found no unresolved bugs affecting safety and efficiency.
evidence.evidenceText: System testing verifies that all functions configured in each module are adequate. No unresolved bugs have been found in this software that affect safety and efficiency.

evidence.claim: The unresolved anomalies list states none.
evidence.evidenceText: Unresolved Anomalies No Problem Description Criticality Reason for Problem Potential Impact None

evidence.claim: Software version 2.3.0.3 for XAS_W is listed as initial release on Nov/15/2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision Date Name Description 2.3.0.3 Nov/15/2022 XAS_W Initial release.

evidence.claim: Firmware versions are listed as 1.1.0.2.23 for A1417MCW and A1717MCW, and 2.0.0.1 for F1417MCW, all initial release on Nov/15/2022.
evidence.evidenceText: Firmware version info ( A1417MCW ) 1.1.0.2.23 Nov/15/2022 FIRMWARE Initial release ... Firmware version info ( A1717MCW ) 1.1.0.2.23 Nov/15/2022 FIRMWARE Initial release ... Firmware version info ( F1417MCW ) 2.0.0.1 Nov/15/2022 FIRMWARE Initial release

reviewFlags.reason: Document date metadata is inconsistent: early pages show Rev. Date Nov/23/2022, later test pages show Rev. Date Nov/10/2022, and revision history pages show Rev. Date Nov/15/2022.
Authority signal CE/MDR is provided in source metadata and file path, but the document text primarily cites FDA guidance and 21 CFR 820.30 Design Controls.
OCR/text quality issues appear in headings and identifiers, including misspellings such as FRIMWARE, Pwr buttin, and inconsistent hazard numbering HA-005 versus HA-0005.
